title: rubus hirtus var. ferox
intro: Rubus hirtus var. ferox, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Indian raspberry, is a type of shrub native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its woody stems and edible fruits. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. ferox include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to have anti-cancer and anti-diabetic effects. Herbal preparations of Rubus hirtus var. ferox include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to make teas, tonics, and other medicinal remedies.
